title: Trisetaria pumila
intro: Trisetaria pumila, also known as short oaten-grass, is a small, perennial grass species native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as respiratory issues and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Trisetaria pumila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Trisetaria pumila include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Trisetaria pumila is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. Trisetaria pumila is further classified into the genus Trisetaria. The species is native to Europe and has been naturalized in parts of Asia and North America. It is a perennial grass that grows in a variety of habitats.
